faults.location: Section 3.4.1 – Signals from Central Module to Taxi module
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.4.1 to reject a taxi-submitted order when no taxis are available. The design only provides a Confirm signal but no rejection/negative acknowledgement for orders submitted by the driver.

faults.location: Section 3.1 – Order_struct
faults.description: Order_struct has no Priority field, but UC 1.2 Task 2 states that orders can be prioritized (some customers are more important than others).

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 – Signals from Central module to user (operator)
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.3.2 to send an estimated wait time to the operator when an order is very close in time. UC 1.2 Variant 3b requires the system to present this estimate.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 – Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: LoginSignal is defined without any parameters. UC 1.4 Task 3 requires that the terminal sends driver information to the central on login, so the LoginSignal must carry at least a driver identification.

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 – Signals from Taxi Module to Central Module
faults.description: LoggedIn(integer) only carries the taxi number. UC 1.4 Task 3 requires driver information (driver ID) to be sent to the central on login; the driver number is missing from this signal.

faults.location: Section 3.4.1 – Signals from Central Module to Taxi module
faults.description: No LoginFailed signal is defined in 3.4.1 (Central→Taxi). UC 1.4 Variant 4b requires the central to reject the driver when the card is not valid; the only login response defined in 3.4.1 is LoginOk.

faults.location: Section 3.2.2 – Signals from the taxi module to the user (taxi driver)
faults.description: No Confirm signal is defined in 3.2.2 to relay the central's order confirmation to the driver. UC 1.5 Task 4 states the central confirms the order; this confirmation must reach the driver via the Taxi module.

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 – Signals from Taxi Module to Central Module
faults.description: Position(integer, integer) only carries taxi number and zone number. UC 1.6 Task 8 requires that when the driver is confident in time of arrival, both arrival zone AND time are sent to the central; the time parameter is missing.

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 – Signals from Taxi Module to Central Module
faults.description: Position(integer, integer) only carries zone number (an integer approximation), not exact coordinates. UC 1.8 Task 3 requires exact coordinates to be sent to the central every 30 seconds during an alarm.

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 – Signals from Central module to user (operator)
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.3.2 to notify the operator that a manually dispatched order has been accepted by the car. UC 1.10 Task 5 requires this notification.

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 – Signals from Taxi Module to Central Module
faults.description: No text message acknowledgement signal is defined in 3.4.2. UC 1.11 Task 4 requires each receiving car to automatically acknowledge reception of a text message to the central.

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 – Signals from Central module to user (operator)
faults.description: No text message delivery confirmation signal is defined in 3.3.2. UC 1.11 Task 5 requires the system to confirm delivery of the text message to the operator.

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 – Signals from Central module to user (operator)
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.3.2 to send a list of available cars to the operator. UC 1.13 Task 2 requires the system to display a list of available cars when the operator selects radio call.

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 – Signals from Central module to user (operator)
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.3.2 to notify the operator that the desired car is not available for voice communication. UC 1.13 Variant 4b requires this negative feedback when no connection can be established.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 – Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.2.1 for the driver to request their own or the car's properties. UC 1.16 Task 1 requires the driver to be able to select viewing properties on the terminal.

faults.location: Section 3.4.1 – Signals from Central Module to Taxi module
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.4.1 for the central to send driver or car property information to the taxi. UC 1.16 Task 2 requires the central to send this information to the car terminal.

faults.location: Section 3.3.1 – Signals from the user (operator) to the central module
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.3.1 for the operator to submit updated driver properties. UC 1.18 Task 3 requires the operator to be able to update and submit driver properties.

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 – Signals from Central module to user (operator)
faults.description: No acknowledgement signal is defined in 3.3.2 for confirming driver property updates to the operator. UC 1.18 Task 4 requires the system to acknowledge the changes.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 – Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: Reset_Alarm(integer) is defined in 3.2.1 as a signal from the driver to the taxi module, allowing the driver to reset the alarm. UC 1.7 Task 3 states that only the operator can reset the alarm.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 – Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: No logout signal is defined in 3.2.1 for the driver to initiate a logout from the terminal. The design defines LoggedOut in 3.4.2 (Taxi→Central) but provides no corresponding driver-initiated signal in the Taxi-User API.

faults.location: Section 3.3.1 – Signals from the user (operator) to the central module
faults.description: The Taxi(integer, integer, integer) signal is described as 'the response' to an operator request, indicating it is a Central→Operator response signal, but it is placed in 3.3.1 (Operator→Central direction) instead of 3.3.2.

faults.location: Section 3.4.1 – Signals from Central Module to Taxi module
faults.description: LoginOk(integer) only carries the taxi number, but the login confirmation must include the driver number per the requirement that login is confirmed by taxi number and driver number. The driver number parameter is missing.

faults.location: Section 3.6 – Communication Protocol (Table 1)
faults.description: The Confirm signal (defined in 3.4.1 as Central→Taxi) is absent from Table 1. All signals transmitted over the communication link between Central and Taxi must have a Message Type Id in Table 1.

faults.location: Section 4.1 – MSC Order (step 1)
faults.description: MSC 4.1 step 1 shows the signal 'Order(New_Order)' from Operator to Central, but section 3.3.1 defines this signal as 'OrderOper(Order_struct, integer)'. The signal name in the MSC is inconsistent with the defined API.

faults.location: Section 4.1 – MSC Order
faults.description: MSC 4.1 is missing the Confirm signal from Central to Taxi after the Ack in step 8. UC 1.5 Task 4 and UC 1.10 Task 4 require the central to confirm the order acceptance back to the taxi.

faults.location: Section 4.2 – MSC Voice (step 5)
faults.description: MSC 4.2 step 5 shows 'Confirm_Voice(Taxi_Nbr)' from Driver to Taxi, but no Confirm_Voice signal is defined in 3.2.1 (Signals from user/driver to the taxi module). This signal is missing from the API definition.

faults.location: Section 4.2 – MSC Voice (step 15)
faults.description: MSC 4.2 step 15 shows 'Start_Voice(Taxi_Nbr)' with only one parameter from Driver to Taxi, but 3.2.1 defines Start_Voice(integer, integer) with two parameters (taxi number and operator number). The MSC is inconsistent with the signal definition.
